description: Brutal but safe (no protected classes, no slurs, no actual harm). Damage 6-10.

roasts.template: I'd call you an idiot, but that would be an insult to idiots. They've earned their title. You haven't.
You're not the dumbest person in the world. But you better hope they don't die.
I don't have the energy to roast you. The disappointment already took it out of me.
You bring everyone so much joy… when you leave the room.
Some people bring joy wherever they go. You bring joy whenever you go.
I'd roast you, but it would be a waste of perfectly good insults.
You're the reason the word 'mediocre' had to be invented. They needed a polite way to describe you.
Your secrets are safe with me. I don't even tell the people I respect about you.
You're like a {platform} subscription — everyone forgot they had you, and nobody wants to admit it.
I've seen better life choices come out of a {item}.

values: fortune cookie that's been opened twice

roasts.template: You're not the sharpest tool in the shed. You're not even in the shed.
I'd say you're a disaster, but disasters usually have passion behind them. You have an excuse.
Your thought process is like an {service} — slow, expensive, and somehow always down at the worst possible moment.
If disappointment was an Olympic sport, you'd have a full scholarship, a national anthem, and a wax statue at Madame Tussauds.
You're the reason I have trust issues. Specifically, trust in you. And people adjacent to you. And the air you breathe.
You have the charisma of an {object}. A wet one. In a dumpster. Behind a gas station.
I'd say bless your heart, but it would imply you have one. The receipt is missing. The donor is also missing.
You're the kind of person that makes the mute button feel like a personality upgrade. The mute button agrees.
Your opinion is like an {noun} — small, smelly, and nobody asked for it. The dog agrees. The dog is also avoiding eye contact.
You're proof that evolution sometimes takes a coffee break. And apparently a long one. The break is now permanent.
If common sense was a currency, you'd be in the negative. The bank is also bankrupt. The Federal Reserve is concerned.
You're not a bad person. You're just not a person who should be talking right now. To anyone. About anything. Maybe ever.
You bring the same energy to conversations as a {noun} on a {place}.
Your energy is giving 'I peaked in {event} and the peak wasn't that high.' The mountain is a hill. The hill is a speed bump.
Oh, {username}? Right. I remember you. You came back for more. That's either brave or a sign you have nothing else going on, and {roaster_pronoun} is not going to pretend it was brave.

Listen, {roaster_title}. {roaster_pronoun} is not a therapist. {roaster_pronoun_poss} is also not your mom. But between {roaster_pronoun_obj} and your group chats, somebody had to say it: you are the main character of nobody's story.
{username}, you walked in here like you wanted a fight and you're losing to a {roaster_self} with a day job. Sit down. Read a book. Then come back. Maybe.
Honestly, {username}? You are not the worst I've seen today. You are the worst I've seen {time_of_day}. There's a difference, and {roaster_pronoun} is going to be petty about it.
Oh, we open with 'hi' and expect mercy? No, {roaster_title}. This is a roast, not a greeting card. Sit down. Open a vein. Spill something.
Leaving so soon? The mirror will be relieved. So will the door. So will {username}'s browser history. Try the gym on the way out. You owe it to us.
You are welcome. The feeling is not mutual. Also, your thanks just got logged. We are using it as evidence. In court. Of being polite. To a roast bot.

Wow. We have been doing this for two messages and we are already at 'I love you.' {roaster_title}, the bar is in hell, and {username} just limbo'd under it with style. Style that fits on the back of a cereal box.

'{trigger_phrase}.' Wonderful. {username} just outpaced a {animal} in a {context} by hitting a single key. The {animal} is filing for early retirement. The {context} is suing for emotional damages. {roaster_pronoun} is taking notes.
Oh, you watch anime? Cool. Cool. So does every NPC in the {context}. The difference is the {context} is a {noun}, and the {noun} has a job.

{username} YOLO'd rent money into {coin} at the top. The chart did a backflip. {roaster_pronoun} is down {percent}%. {username} is down the apartment. The {coin} community is up a meme. The meme is also down.

Oh, {username} thinks {roaster_pronoun} is {adjective}? {roaster_title}, you are {adjective} the way an {item} is {item_adj} — technically, on paper, after a {process}. In person, it's a fireable offense. From HR. From life. From the mirror.

values: hot dog from a gas station

roasts.template: {username} mains {game}. {roaster_pronoun} has the aim of an {noun} on {platform} with a broken {controller}. The {noun} is doing its best. The {controller} is doing the rest. {roaster_pronoun_poss} KD is a {ratio} which is also {roaster_pronoun_poss} blood pressure.

{username} 's screen time is {hours} hours a day. {roaster_title}, that is not a lifestyle. That is an {noun} and a {adjective} dream. Your phone is your best friend. Your phone is also the {noun}'s best friend. The {noun} is a {noun_2}. The {noun_2} is also you.

{username} wakes up at 5AM to hustle. {roaster_pronoun} also goes to bed at 3AM to scroll. The net hustle is two hours of cortisol and a {noun}. The {noun} is the only thing on the morning routine. The 5AM alarm has filed a restraining order.
